# RefactorKit v0.4.0 Release Notes

RefactorKit `v0.4.0` publishes the hardened Java and managed-transaction
foundation developed after `v0.3.0`. API baseline remains `0.2`; stable `1.0` is
deliberately deferred to the supreme multi-language roadmap.

## Highlights

- Qualified TX-001–TX-018 managed-file transaction contract with lock-scoped
  snapshot validation, durable WAL, atomic staged replacement, recovery,
  conflict-safe rollback, corruption quarantine, filesystem metadata restoration,
  process-kill/torn-write/fault acceptance, authorization and diagnostics gates.
- Eclipse JDT 3.44/JLS25 analysis for Java 8–25 with module/dependency-bounded
  classpaths, generated-source refusal and structured `JDT_BINDING`, `STRUCTURAL`,
  and review-only `LEXICAL_FALLBACK` evidence.
- Compiler diagnostics with stable range/evidence/category and exact staged source
  overlays that prevent stale-workspace false regressions.
- New deterministic `format-file` operation using Eclipse JDT project preferences
  or versioned defaults, minimal edits, idempotence, BOM/line-ending preservation,
  staged diagnostics and preview/apply/rollback.
- Corrected CLI boolean option parsing, machine-readable API `0.2` inventory,
  protocol request limits, pending-plan bounds and reference-workspace budgets.
- Reproducible runtime ZIP settings, SPDX JSON SBOM, GitHub build provenance and
  SBOM attestations in the release workflow.
- New long-range roadmap: deep IDE-grade language adapters through Clojure before
  stable `1.0`, with every newly approved capability assigned before `1.0`.

## Compatibility and migration

See `docs/releases/v0.4.0-migration.md`. Existing API `0.2` workflows remain
compatible. Scripts that incorrectly passed values to boolean CLI flags must use
the documented positional/option layout.

## Artifacts

The release workflow replaces the placeholders below in the published body.

- Source tag: _TBD before release_
- Release commit: _TBD before release_
- Runtime zip: `refactorkit-runtime-0.4.0-linux-x86_64.zip`
  - Final URL: _TBD after release artifact is published_
- Checksum file: `refactorkit-runtime-0.4.0-linux-x86_64.zip.sha256`
  - Final SHA-256: _TBD after release artifact is built_
- SBOM: `refactorkit-runtime.spdx.json`
- Attestations: GitHub artifact build provenance and SBOM attestation

Verification:

```bash
sha256sum -c refactorkit-runtime-0.4.0-linux-x86_64.zip.sha256
unzip -q refactorkit-runtime-0.4.0-linux-x86_64.zip -d /tmp/refactorkit-v0.4.0
RK=/tmp/refactorkit-v0.4.0/refactorkit/bin/refactorkit
env -u JAVA_HOME "$RK" --version
env -u JAVA_HOME "$RK" --help
env -u JAVA_HOME "$RK" scan samples/java-maven-simple
```

## Acceptance

- [x] full build and protocol suites;
- [x] 23/23 golden cases;
- [x] packaged embedded-runtime smoke;
- [x] Java compatibility and diagnostics acceptance;
- [x] formatter idempotence/configuration/BOM/refusal acceptance;
- [x] qualified transactionality audit;
- [x] reference 1,000-file budgets;
- [x] repeat archive hash equality before final version stamping;
- [ ] tag CI, final checksum, SBOM/attestations and independent downloaded-artifact smoke (completed by/after publication).

## Known limitations

- API `0.2` remains beta; this is not stable API `1.0`.
- Packaged runtime target is Linux x86_64.
- Java range/changed-lines formatting is not included yet.
- Native LSP edits are client-managed; use managed preview/apply for rollback.
- Synchronous API `0.2` servers do not advertise in-flight cancellation.
- External build scripts, unknown framework strings, reflection and generated
  sources retain conservative warning/refusal behavior.

## Rollback

Managed workspace changes remain rollbackable:

```bash
refactorkit patch rollback <transaction-id> --root <workspace>
```

If the release must be withdrawn, keep the immutable tag, mark the GitHub release
withdrawn, document the incident and publish a new patch version.
